title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For High Blood Pressure
intro: Herbal teas for High blood pressure are a type of herbal remedy made from plants and flowers that have been used for centuries to help manage and treat hypertension. These teas are effective in treating high blood pressure because they are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other compounds that help to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce stress, all of which contribute to lowering blood pressure. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat high blood pressure include hibiscus tea, which has been shown to lower systolic blood pressure; rosehip tea, which is rich in potassium and helps to reduce blood pressure; dandelion tea, which acts as a diuretic to help remove excess fluids from the body; chamomile tea, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce stress; peppermint tea, which can help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation; passionflower tea, which has a calming effect on the nervous system; and yarrow tea, which helps to regulate blood pressure by reducing blood vessel constriction. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can find a natural and effective way to manage and treat high blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow. The bioactive constituents of ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to their potential in managing high blood pressure. By reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, ginkgo biloba teas may help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Regular consumption of ginkgo biloba teas may also help to promote overall cardiovascular health and reduce the severity of hypertension symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the Ginkgo biloba leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea per day, preferably before meals, to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Clinical and experimental pharmacology & physiology", Ginkgo biloba teas for high blood pressure appear to have an anti-hypertensive effect, which is most prominent in the daytime and can help attenuate the development of hypertension in hypertensive rats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help lower high blood pressure, but possible side effects include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its ability to thin the blood and interact with other medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, it's recommended to start with a low dose, gradually increase it, and avoid taking it with other medications that also thin the blood, such as aspirin or warfarin, to minimize the risk of excessive bleeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2 times a day with meals.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup of tea.
Gradually increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup of tea after 1 week.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage if necessary.
Consult doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea if taking medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Crataegus monogyna teas have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to inhibit the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key regulator of blood pressure. This herbal preparation has been shown to be effective in reducing systolic and diastolic blood pressure in patients with hypertension, making it a potential natural remedy for managing this condition. The benefits of Crataegus monogyna teas in treating high blood pressure include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, and a decrease in the need for antihypertensive medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the solids. Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crataegus monogyna teas can be used to treat high blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and changes in heart rate, which can be more pronounced in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions. When using Crataegus monogyna teas to treat high blood pressure, it is essential to monitor your blood pressure regularly and be cautious of interactions with other medications, as well as to avoid consuming excessive amounts, which can lead to over-sedation or allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Crataegus monogyna tea for high blood pressure.
Start with 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 1 week.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor.
Increase dosage to 2 cups of tea, 3 times a day, if needed.
Maintain dosage for 6-8 weeks under doctor's supervision.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their relaxing and calming properties. The valerenic acid and isovaleric acid present in these teas help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common contributors to high blood pressure. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, Valeriana officinalis teas can help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat high blood pressure include reduced risk of heart disease, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the need for medication.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root in a teapot.
Steep the Valeriana officinalis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating high blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and also to avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, as it may impair cognitive function and reaction time.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Valeriana officinalis for high blood pressure.
Start with 1/2 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 2-3 days.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage to 1 cup, 2-3 times a day.
Continue using tea for 2-4 weeks to assess its effectiveness.
Work with doctor to adjust dosage or add other treatments as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to help manage high blood pressure due to their calming and relaxing properties, which can help reduce stress and promote better sleep. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been found to have a vasodilatory effect, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, ultimately contributing to a reduction in blood pressure. The regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may help to lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure levels, reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat high blood pressure include reduced anxiety, improved sleep quality, and a lower risk of heart-related complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Lavandula angustifolia leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup with the Lavandula leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed for high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help lower blood pressure, but it may cause dizziness or lightheadedness due to a sudden drop in blood pressure. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it's essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase it to avoid interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners, and to avoid consuming it before or after surgery.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Lavandula angustifolia for high blood pressure.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia in a cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals.
Gradually increase the dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed, under medical supervision.
Continue using the tea for 6-8 weeks and monitor blood pressure regularly with your healthcare provider.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their potent bioactive constituents and therapeutic properties. The bioactive compounds present in ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and relaxing blood vessels. The anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties of ginger tea contribute to its ability to alleviate high blood pressure, making it a popular natural remedy for hypertensive patients. Regular consumption of ginger tea has been shown to have numerous benefits, including reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Peel the ginger roots and slice them thinly into small pieces.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of sliced ginger to a cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the ginger and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups per day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Alternative therapies in health and medicine", Zingiber officinale teas for high blood pressure may be beneficial as a result of a study that found a significant decrease in blood pressure in rats treated with a combination of Tribulus terrestris and Zingiber officinale extracts, indicating its potential use in the treatment of hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to help lower blood pressure, but consuming them excessively may lead to side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to their active compound, gingerol, which can cause stomach irritation. Precautions should be taken when using Zingiber officinale teas to treat high blood pressure, including avoiding consumption in large amounts, during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and in individuals with bleeding disorders or taking blood-thinning medications, as it may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for high blood pressure.
Take 1/2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 1-2 cups per day.
Take Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day, after breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. The bioactive constituents, including carnosic acid and ursolic acid, contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key regulator of blood pressure. By reducing the levels of ACE, Rosmarinus officinalis teas help to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, thereby reducing the risk of related complications such as heart disease and stroke. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been associated with numerous benefits, including improved blood pressure regulation, reduced inflammation, and enhanced antioxidant activity, making it a promising herbal remedy for the management of high blood pressure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture with a fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to make the tea sweeter.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, to help manage high blood pressure.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Rosmarinus officinalis tea as a treatment for high blood pressure, especially if you are taking medications.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to help lower high blood pressure, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as starting with small amounts to assess tolerance and avoiding use if pregnant or breastfeeding due to potential effects on the fetus or baby.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Rosmarinus officinalis tea for high blood pressure.
Start with 1/2 cup of tea 2-3 times a day for 1 week.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage to 1 cup 2-3 times a day.
Continue using 1 cup 2-3 times a day for 2-3 months.
Check with doctor for further use and dosage adjustments.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas, also known as passionflower teas, have been used to treat high blood pressure due to their calming and sedative properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce anxiety and stress, which are major contributors to high blood pressure, by promoting relaxation and reducing sympathetic nervous system activity. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, in Passiflora incarnata teas contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the release of stress hormones like adrenaline and cortisol, and improving blood vessel function. The benefits of using Passiflora incarnata teas to treat high blood pressure include reduced blood pressure readings, improved cardiovascular health, and a decrease in the risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Passiflora incarnata flowers in the tea infuser or cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup using the tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the Passiflora incarnata tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can help alleviate high blood pressure symptoms, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties, as well as interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as monitoring liver and kidney function, avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and starting with low doses to minimize potential side effects like headaches and fatigue.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Passiflora incarnata tea for high blood pressure.
Start with 1/4 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 1-2 weeks.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage to 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, if needed.
Take tea 30 minutes before meals to help regulate blood pressure.
Continue using tea for 2-3 months, then reassess and adjust dosage as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their calming and relaxing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing stress and anxiety, which are major contributors to high blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, including flavonoids and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which help to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat high blood pressure include a reduction in blood pressure readings, improved cardiovascular health, and a decrease in the risk of heart disease and stroke.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical design", Melissa officinalis teas for high blood pressure have shown potential cardiovascular activity and are among the most effective plants suggested by Avicenna in treating cardiovascular diseases, including high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to help treat high blood pressure, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, avoid consuming it before bedtime to minimize dizziness, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to the potential for drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Melissa officinalis tea for high blood pressure.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides and polysaccharides, have been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and reducing oxidative stress. As a result, regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas has been found to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The benefits of using Panax ginseng teas to treat high blood pressure include improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the ginseng root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the ginseng root.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Endocrine, metabolic & immune disorders drug targets", Panax ginseng teas for high blood pressure have been recognized for their blood pressure lowering abilities, with different types of extracts and isolated molecules demonstrating their efficiency in improving blood pressure either experimentally or clinically.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can help lower blood pressure in some individuals, but possible side effects may include insomnia, restlessness, and rapid heartbeat due to its stimulatory effects on the body. To use Panax ginseng teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it before bedtime or with other stimulants to minimize potential interactions and side effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Panax ginseng tea for high blood pressure.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried ginseng per cup of boiling water, 1-2 times a day.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup of boiling water, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of Panax ginseng tea per day, for 6-8 weeks, and monitor blood pressure.
Adjust dosage and frequency with doctor's guidance, based on blood pressure readings.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, a traditional herbal preparation, have been used to help manage high blood pressure due to its vasodilatory and diuretic properties. The bioactive constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and increase urine production, thereby alleviating the symptoms of high blood pressure. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often associated with high blood pressure. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for high blood pressure include a natural and non-invasive approach to management, reduced risk of medication side effects, and improved overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root. Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally before meals, to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Plants (Basel, Switzerland), Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for high blood pressure may be beneficial due to their active plant component 18β-glycyrrhetinic acid (18βGA), which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and has potential in managing pulmonary arterial hypertension, a condition related to high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat high blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as water retention, increased blood pressure, and low levels of potassium, especially when consumed in large amounts or for extended periods. Precautions should be taken when using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat high blood pressure, including monitoring blood pressure levels, limiting consumption to recommended amounts, and being mindful of potential interactions with other medications that may affect potassium levels.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their ability to improve cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, help to treat high blood pressure by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE). The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Curcuma longa teas help to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been shown to provide benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced blood pressure, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried turmeric roots (Curcuma longa), 2 cups of water, and a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Rinse the dried turmeric roots with water and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped turmeric roots and water in a saucepan. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a tea infuser or cheesecloth into a cup. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down.
Drink 1 cup of the turmeric tea 2-3 times a day. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but do not add sugar.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of complementary & integrative medicine, Curcuma longa teas for high blood pressure have been found to cause a decline in elevated blood pressure of hypertensive rats, indicating a potential cardioprotective effect in managing hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be a natural remedy for high blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, such as nausea, diarrhea, or stomach upset, and allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. To use Curcuma longa teas safely, take precautions like starting with a low dose, monitoring your blood pressure regularly, and avoiding it if you have gallstones or are pregnant or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas, also known as ashwagandha tea, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and balance its physiological response. The bioactive constituents of ashwagandha tea, including withanolides and alkaloids, help to reduce blood pressure by improving cardiovascular function and reducing inflammation. By reducing oxidative stress and modulating the renin-angiotensin system, ashwagandha tea helps to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. The benefits of using ashwagandha tea to treat high blood pressure include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved blood lipid profiles, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Withania somnifera roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera roots and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Withania somnifera roots and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the Withania somnifera tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Withania somnifera teas for high blood pressure may be beneficial as they have been reported to show blood glucose-lowering and diuretic effects in humans, comparable to daonil.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Withania somnifera teas can have several side effects when used to treat high blood pressure, including dizziness, headache, and stomach upset due to its diuretic properties. To minimize these risks, individuals should be cautious of their body's response and gradually adjust the dosage, also avoiding taking it too close to bedtime or when combined with other medications that lower blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to help manage high blood pressure due to their diuretic and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and taraxasterol, help to treat high blood pressure by reducing blood vessel constriction and promoting the excretion of excess fluids and electrolytes. Regular consumption of dandelion tea may also help to lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure levels, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat high blood pressure include reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular health, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers. Wash them with water.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils. Remove from heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of the Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to the boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine mesh sieve. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure. Consult a doctor before using as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study on Nutrients, Taraxacum officinale teas for high blood pressure may be beneficial due to the plant's rich source of phenolic compounds with antiplatelet, anticoagulant, or antioxidant activities that can help prevent or mitigate cardiovascular diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to help lower blood pressure, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions. When using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat high blood pressure, it's essential to be cautious, especially if you have stomach problems or are prone to bleeding, as excessive consumption may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Olea europaea teas, derived from the leaves of the olive tree, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their unique properties that help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation. The bioactive constituents, including oleuropein, tyrosol, and hydroxytyrosol, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and promoting vasodilation. Regular consumption of Olea europaea teas has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure, such as dizziness, headaches, and fatigue, by improving cardiovascular health and reducing oxidative stress. Overall, the benefits of using Olea europaea teas to treat high blood pressure include improved blood flow, reduced blood pressure, and a lower risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Olea europaea leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the leaves and put them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the leaves in the tea infuser or cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Olea europaea teas for high blood pressure have been found to have a dose-dependent relaxation effect on rat aorta rings, with IC50 values indicating that the aqueous extracts of Olea europaea leaves induce relaxation at a lower concentration compared to a polyherbal preparation (Ifanosine) made from it, suggesting potential antihypertensive effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Olea europaea teas can help lower high blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its high polyphenol content. To use Olea europaea teas safely, precautions should be taken, such as starting with a low dose, avoiding it with certain medications like blood thinners, and monitoring blood pressure closely to avoid over-suppression, which may lead to hypotension.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to help treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and rosmarinic acid, are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and calcium channel-blocking properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Salvia officinalis teas may help to lower blood pressure by reducing vascular resistance and improving endothelial function. Regular consumption of Salvia officinalis teas has been associated with various benefits, including reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular health, and a lower risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the leaves from the water using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
Consult with a doctor or a healthcare professional before using Salvia officinalis tea as a treatment for high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Acta physiologica et pharmacologica Bulgarica, Salvia officinalis teas for high blood pressure may be effective in lowering blood pressure due to the moderate but prolonged lowering of blood pressure caused by the aqueous-alcohol extract of Salvia officinalis when applied intravenously and duodenally in cats.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat high blood pressure, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, take the recommended dosage and avoid consuming them if you have kidney or liver problems, as they may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory and antioxidant properties. These teas help to lower blood pressure by promoting relaxation of blood vessels, improving circulation, and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus, including flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and reducing inflammation. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus teas has been found to provide benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root.
Add the root to a tea infuser or directly to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea per day, as needed, to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Alternative medicine review : a journal of clinical therapeutic, Astragalus membranaceus teas may have therapeutic benefit for the treatment of high blood pressure due to its in vitro antioxidant activity, which is the mechanism by which it affords its cardioprotective benefit.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to potentially lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches in some users. To take precautions when using this herbal preparation, one should be cautious with its interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, and avoid consuming it in excessive amounts, especially with other stimulants or sedatives, to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, derived from the bark of Cinnamomum verum, a species of cinnamon, have been used in traditional medicine to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in Cinnamomum verum teas, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, help to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce blood pressure. Regular consumption of Cinnamomum verum teas has been shown to help treat high blood pressure by reducing systolic and diastolic blood pressure, as well as lowering the risk of cardiovascular diseases. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas for high blood pressure treatment include a reduction in blood pressure, improved cardiovascular health, and a lower risk of stroke and heart failure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of Cinnamomum verum powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Cinnamomum verum powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to combine.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink 1 cup of the Cinnamomum verum tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be used to help alleviate high blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, lightheadedness, or stomach upset in some individuals due to its blood-thinning properties. To use Cinnamomum verum teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it with other medications that may interact with its compounds, and be cautious when standing up or changing positions to minimize the risk of dizziness.

preparations.herb_desc: Trigonella foenum-graecum teas, also known as fenugreek tea, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to its diuretic properties that help to reduce fluid retention in the body. This herbal preparation helps to alleviate high blood pressure by promoting relaxation of blood vessels, improving circulation, and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents present in fenugreek tea, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-hypertensive effects that contribute to its blood pressure-lowering properties. Regular consumption of fenugreek tea has been shown to provide benefits in managing high blood pressure, including reduced blood pressure levels, improved cardiovascular health, and a lower risk of cardiovascular complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of Trigonella foenum-graecum seeds.
Measure out 1 cup of water in a teapot or mug.
Add 1 tablespoon of Trigonella foenum-graecum seeds to the water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the seeds to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups per day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), Trigonella foenum-graecum teas for high blood pressure may be beneficial due to the presence of flavonoids and other glucose-lowering active constituents that have been found to be useful in diabetes associated with hypertension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Trigonella foenum-graecum teas can be used to help treat high blood pressure, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Trigonella foenum-graecum teas to treat high blood pressure, it is essential to be cautious and avoid consuming it in excess, as this may exacerbate the condition, and also to monitor one's blood pressure regularly to prevent over-lowering of blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus teas, also known as bilberry teas, have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The anthocyanins present in these teas help to relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and improve circulation, ultimately contributing to the alleviation of this ailment. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium myrtillus teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, work synergistically to counteract the effects of high blood pressure, making it an effective herbal preparation for its treatment. By incorporating Vaccinium myrtillus teas into one's diet, individuals can benefit from reduced blood pressure levels, improved cardiovascular health, and a lower risk of associated complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium myrtillus berries and clean them.
Use 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil in a pot.
Add the Vaccinium myrtillus berries to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium myrtillus teas can help lower blood pressure in some individuals, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its diuretic properties. Precautions to take when using Vaccinium myrtillus teas to treat high blood pressure include starting with low doses, avoiding consumption in people with kidney problems, and being mindful of potential interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas have been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Rauvolfia serpentina, including reserpine and ajmalicine, help to inhibit the reabsorption of catecholamines, thereby reducing sympathetic nervous activity and lowering blood pressure. Regular consumption of Rauvolfia serpentina teas may also help to reduce peripheral resistance and improve cardiac output, making it an effective herbal preparation for treating high blood pressure. The benefits of using Rauvolfia serpentina teas to treat high blood pressure include reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, improved overall cardiovascular health, and a natural alternative to pharmaceutical medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rauvolfia serpentina root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey or sugar to taste (optional).
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Natural products and bioprospecting, Rauvolfia serpentina teas for high blood pressure have been studied as a potential treatment option, although the study does not provide specific details about its effects on high blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rauvolfia serpentina teas can be used to treat high blood pressure, but possible side effects include drowsiness, dizziness, and nausea, as well as potential interactions with other medications that lower blood pressure. Precautions when using Rauvolfia serpentina teas include starting with a low dose, monitoring blood pressure regularly, and avoiding use in people with kidney or liver disease, as these individuals may be more susceptible to the herb's effects.

preparation_study: According to International journal of molecular sciences, Ginkgo biloba teas for high blood pressure have not been evaluated in the provided study, as it only mentions an evaluation of the cardioprotective and antihypertensive effects of various phytotherapy products, but does not provide information about the effects of Ginkgo biloba teas specifically.
intro_study: According to "Advanced pharmaceutical bulletin", teas for high blood pressure may be beneficial due to medicinal herbs having several active substances with pharmacological and prophylactic properties that can be used in the treatment of hypertension.
